title: Design Handoff JSON (design-handoff.json)
description: Independent validation schema for the design-handoff.json file produced by javafx-designer. This file is consumed by javafx-developer (Step 4) to replace built-in FXML/CSS templates with designer-produced prototypes. Includes metadata fields (design_version, project, created_at, architecture_pattern) for cross-skill handoff consistency.

design_version.description: Designer skill version — must match SKILL.md metadata.version

architecture_pattern.description: Architecture pattern from architecture-handoff.json (if consumed) or inferred from design scope. Used to align FXML structure with the target architecture (e.g., MVVM requires View-ViewModel separation in FXML).

screens.description: Screen prototypes with FXML paths and control lists. Developer uses these FXML files instead of its built-in templates.

fxml_path.description: Relative path to the FXML prototype file (e.g., 'design/fxml/main-view.fxml')

controller_class.description: Controller class name for this screen (e.g., 'MainController')

controls.description: List of fx:id controls defined in this screen's FXML. Developer verifies these match @FXML fields in the generated controller.

themes.description: CSS theme files. Developer copies these to src/main/resources/css/ instead of using built-in CSS templates.

light.description: Path to light theme CSS file

dark.description: Path to dark theme CSS file (optional)

icons.description: Icon configuration. Developer adds Ikonli dependency to pom.xml and configures icon font loading based on this.

interaction_flow.description: Path to the Mermaid interaction flow diagram file. Developer uses this to implement navigation logic between screens.

fx_id_consistency.description: FXML fx:id must match controller @FXML field names exactly

css_resource_paths.description: CSS file paths relative to resources/ directory

icon_dependencies.description: Maven/Gradle dependencies needed for icons (e.g., Ikonli)
